texts.orig: [193] K.H. Yu, F. Can, P. Ergenekon, Nitric oxide and nitrite removal by partial denitrifying hollow-fiber membrane biofilm reactor coupled with nitrous oxide generation as energy recovery, Environ. Technol. 43 (19) (2022) 2934–2947,
texts.text: [193] K.H. Yu, F. Can, P. Ergenekon, Nitric oxide and nitrite removal by partial denitrifying hollow-fiber membrane biofilm reactor coupled with nitrous oxide generation as energy recovery, Environ. Technol. 43 (19) (2022) 2934–2947,

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This study aims to treat gaseous nitric oxide (NO) by hollow-fibre membrane biofilm reactor (HFMBfR) in the presence of nitrite (NO− 2 ) and evaluate nitrous oxide (N2O) emissions formed as an intermediate product during the denitrification process. In the first stage of the study, the HFMBfR was operated by feeding only gaseous NO as the nitrogen source. During this period, the best performance was achieved with 92% NO removal efficiency (RE). In the second stage, both NO gas and NO− 2 were supplied to the system, and 91% NO and 99% NO− 2 reduction were achieved simultaneously.
texts.text: This study aims to treat gaseous nitric oxide (NO) by hollow-fibre membrane biofilm reactor (HFMBfR) in the presence of nitrite (NO− 2 ) and evaluate nitrous oxide (N2O) emissions formed as an intermediate product during the denitrification process. In the first stage of the study, the HFMBfR was operated by feeding only gaseous NO as the nitrogen source. During this period, the best performance was achieved with 92% NO removal efficiency (RE). In the second stage, both NO gas and NO− 2 were supplied to the system, and 91% NO and 99% NO− 2 reduction were achieved simultaneously.
